volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Curculio (Section 2.3.295 - Section 2.3.370b)

sections.label: Section 2.3.295
sections.original: ex unoquoque eorum exciam crépitum polentarium .
sections.translation: from every single one of them I ’ ll squeeze out a belch from their pearled-barley diet [*] . And then those servants of your dainty townsmen [*] , who are playing at catch-ball in the road , both throwers and catchers , all of them I ’ ll pitch under foot . Would they avoid a mishap , why then , let them keep at home .

sections.label: Section 2.3.299
sections.original: Recte hic monstrat , si imperare possit . nam ita nunc mos viget ,
sections.translation: He points out aright , he only requires that he should speak with authority ; for such manners are in vogue in the present day ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.300
sections.original: ita nunc servitiumst : profecto modus haberi non potest .
sections.translation: such at present are the slaves ; really , control cannot be held over them .

sections.label: Section 2.3.301
sections.original: Ecquis est qui mihi commonstret Phaedromum genium meum ?
sections.translation: Is there any one , I wonder , who can point out to me Phaedromus , my good Genius ? The matter is of such pressing nature , I really must meet with the man this instant .

sections.label: Section 2.3.303
sections.translation: He ’ s looking for you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.303b
sections.original: Quid si adeamus ? heus , Curculio , te volo .
sections.translation: What if we accost him ? ( Aloud .) Hallo ! Curculio , I want you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.304
sections.translation: Who ’ s calling me ? Who ’ s mentioning my name ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.304b
sections.translation: One who wishes to meet with you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.305
sections.original: Haud magis me cupis quam ego te cupio .
sections.translation: You don ’ t wish more for me than I wish for you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.305b
sections.translation: O my own ready occasion , Curculio , much longed-for , greetings to you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.306b
sections.translation: Greetings to you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.306c
sections.translation: I ’ m glad that you have arrived safe ; give me your right hand . How stand my hopes ? Troth now , prithee , do speak out .

sections.label: Section 2.3.308b
sections.original: Eloquere , te óbsecro , ubi súnt meae ?
sections.translation: To you , troth now , prithee , do speak out , how stand my own . ( Makes curious gestures .)

sections.label: Section 2.3.309
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.309b
sections.translation: A dimness is beginning to come , my knees are failing through fasting .

sections.label: Section 2.3.310
sections.translation: I ’ faith , through lassitude , I think .

sections.label: Section 2.3.310b
sections.translation: Support me , prithee , do support me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.311
sections.original: Viden ut expálluit ? datin isti sellam , ubi assidat , cito
sections.translation: See how pale he has turned ; will you give him a seat , for him to be seated at once , and an ewer with some water ? Will you make haste , this very instant ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.312b
sections.translation: I ’ m faint .

sections.label: Section 2.3.313
sections.translation: Would you like some water ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.313b
sections.original: Si frustulenta est , da , obsecro hercle , obsorbeam .
sections.translation: If it ’ s full of bits [*] of meat , prithee , give it me to swallow down , i ’ faith .

sections.label: Section 2.3.314
sections.translation: Woe be to that head of yours .

sections.label: Section 2.3.314b
sections.original: Obsecro hercle , facite ventum ut gaudeam .
sections.translation: Troth now , prithee do give me cause to rejoice at my arrival [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.3.315
sections.translation: By all means .

sections.label: Section 2.3.315b
sections.translation: Prithee , what ’ s this you ’ re about .

sections.label: Section 2.3.315c
sections.translation: Some air .

sections.label: Section 2.3.315d
sections.translation: Really , for my part , I don ’ t want a breath to be raised .

sections.label: Section 2.3.316b
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.316c
sections.translation: To eat , that I may rejoice on my arrival .

sections.label: Section 2.3.317
sections.translation: May Jupiter and the Deities confound you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.317b
sections.translation: I ’ m quite undone ; I can hardly see ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.318
sections.original: gramarum habeo dentes plenos , lippiunt fauces fame ,
sections.translation: my mouth is bitter ; my teeth , I find , are blunted [*] ; my jaws are clammy through fasting ; with my entrails thus lank with abstinence from food am I come .

sections.label: Section 2.3.320
sections.translation: You shall eat something just now .

sections.label: Section 2.3.320b
sections.original: Nolo hercle aliquid : certum quam aliquid mavolo .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I don ’ t want something ; I ’ d rather have what ’ s fixed for certain , than your something .

sections.label: Section 2.3.321
sections.original: Immo si scias , reliquiae quae sint .
sections.translation: Aye , but if you only knew what has been put by for you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.321b
sections.translation: I ’ d very much like to know where it is ; for really it ’ s necessary for it and my teeth to make acquaintance .

sections.label: Section 2.3.323
sections.translation: A gammon of bacon , a sow ’ s stomach , some udder and kernels of the throat .

sections.label: Section 2.3.323b
sections.translation: What , all this do you say ? Perhaps you mean that they are in the flesh-market ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.324b
sections.translation: In the dishes , I mean ; they ’ ve been got ready for you , since we knew that you were about to arrive .

sections.label: Section 2.3.325b
sections.translation: Take care you don ’ t be fooling me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.326b
sections.original: Ita me amabit quám ego amo , ut ego haud mentior .
sections.translation: So may the fair one love me whom I love , I don ’ t say what ’ s false . But as to what I sent you upon I ’ m none the wiser yet .

sections.label: Section 2.3.327b
sections.translation: I ’ ve brought back nothing .

sections.label: Section 2.3.328
sections.translation: You ’ ve undone me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.328b
sections.original: Invenire possum , si mi operam datis .
sections.translation: I can find something , if you ’ ll give me your attention . After , at your request , I had set out , I arrived in Caria ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.330
sections.original: video tuom sodalem , argenti rogo uti faciat copiam .
sections.translation: I saw your friend ; I asked him to make me a loan of some money . In answer , you were to know that he was willing to oblige you ; he didn ’ t wish to disappoint you , as it is only proper that a person who is a friend should be ready , and should assist his friend . In a few words he answered me , and quite in confidence , that he also was in the same extreme want of money as yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.3.335
sections.translation: By your words you ensure my undoing .

sections.label: Section 2.3.335b
sections.translation: Why no ; I ’ m saving you , and wish you to be saved . After this answer was given me , I went away from him to the Forum , in sorrow that I had applied to him in vain . By accident I espied a military officer ; this person I accosted , and as I approached I saluted him . Save you , said he to me , took my right hand , drew me aside , and asked me why I had come to Caria .

sections.label: Section 2.3.340
sections.original: dico me illo advenisse animi causa . ibi me interrogat ,
sections.translation: I said that I had come there for the sake of amusement . Upon this he asked me whether I knew a certain Lyco , a banker of Epidaurus . I said I knew him . Well , and the Procurer Cappadox ? I answered yes , that I had seen him . But what do you want of him ? said I . Because , said he , I bought of him a girl for thirty minae , her clothes and golden jewels too ; and for these last ten minae more are added .

sections.label: Section 2.3.345
sections.original: dédisti tu argentum ? inquam . “ ímmo ápud trapezitam situm est
sections.translation: Have you paid the money ? said I . No , said he ; it is lodged with this Lyco the banker , whom I was mentioning , and I ’ ve instructed him that the person who should bring a letter sealed with my own ring , to him he was to give his services , that he might receive the damsel , with her jewels of gold and her clothes , from the Procurer . After he told me this , I was going away from him . At once he called me back ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.350
sections.original: vocat me ad cenam ; religio fuit , denegare nolui .
sections.translation: invited me to dinner ; it was a point of conscience , I was unwilling to refuse him . What if we go off home , and take our places at table ? said he . The suggestion pleased me ; it is neither proper to lengthen out the day , nor to curtail the night . Everything was prepared , and we , for whom it was prepared , were at our places . After we had dined and well drunk , he asked for the dice to be fetched him .

sections.label: Section 2.3.355
sections.original: provocat me in aleam , ut ego ludam : pono pallium ;
sections.translation: He challenged me to play with him a game of hazard . I staked my cloak , he staked his ring against it ; he called on the name [*] of Planesium .

sections.label: Section 2.3.357
sections.translation: What , my mistress ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.357b
sections.translation: Be silent a while . He threw a most losing cast [*] . I took up the dice , and invoked Hercules as my genial patron [*] ; I threw a first-rate cast [*] , and pledged him in a bumping cup ; in return he drank it off ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.360
sections.original: caput deponit , condormiscit . ego ei subduco anulum ,
sections.translation: reclined his head , and fell fast asleep . I slily took away from him the ring , and took my legs quietly from off the couch , so that the Captain mightn ’ t perceive it . The servants enquired whither I was going ; I said that I was going whither persons when full are wont to go . When I beheld the door , at once on the instant I betook myself away from the place .

sections.label: Section 2.3.364
sections.translation: I commend you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.364b
sections.original: Laudato quando illud quod cupis effecero .
sections.translation: Commend me when I ’ ve brought this thing about which you desire . Now let ’ s go indoors , that we may seal the letter .

sections.label: Section 2.3.365b
sections.translation: Do I delay you ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.366
sections.original: Atque aliquid prius obstrudamus , pernam , sumen , glandium ,
sections.translation: But let ’ s cram down something first , the gammon , the udder , and the kernels ; these are the foundations for the stomach , with bread and roast beef , a good-sized cup and a capacious pot , that counsel enough may be forthcoming . Do you , yourself , seal the letter ; he ’ ll do the honors ( pointing to PALINURUS ) while I am eating .

sections.label: Section 2.3.370
sections.original: dicam quem ad modum conscribas . sequere me hac intro .
sections.translation: I ’ ll dictate after what fashion you ’ re to write . Follow me this way , in-doors .

sections.label: Section 2.3.370b
sections.translation: I follow .
